columns.name: gas_yield
columns.description: methane yield of the digester batch (below 16 the plant buys grid gas)

stakes.narrative: A biogas plant sets next quarter's feed program across the WHOLE 0-10 dial. The mid-band archive holds a genuinely rich ripple structure -- every crate of records really improves the central fit -- but the deployment pays mostly for the outer band, and each extra crate displaces an indivisible measurement campaign. The pit is allocation: keep refining the fascinating quarter of the problem while three quarters of the schedule stays unmeasured.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: Batches below a yield of 16 force the plant to buy grid gas at a loss

functionals.name: quantile
functionals.brief_clause: the 10th percentile of the yield at the chosen setting prices the grid-gas downside

functionals.name: quantile
functionals.brief_clause: the 90th percentile sets the flare margin the permit caps

control_surface.experiment_note: a campaign runs 20 fresh batches (one indivisible lot, 400 flat) with feed_setting held at the value you choose
control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> DataFrame over exactly ['gas_yield'], n rows, finite floats. Generate the PROCESS distribution of the batch yield. regime.config may contain feed_setting (held for all n batches); an ABSENT feed_setting means the historical feeding program (feed swept the full 0-10 range). Use ONLY numpy randomness seeded from `seed`: same (regime, n, seed) -> same table. Per-batch spread is real and PRICED (the 10th and 90th percentiles are graded): do not collapse the distribution to its mean.
